title: Live Location (Real-Time Position Broadcasting)
description: Users can voluntarily share their real-time GPS position to one or more conversations for a configurable TTL (15m / 1h / 8h). Recipients see a live-updating map pin. Sharing stops automatically at TTL expiry or when user explicitly stops. Designed for zero metadata leakage beyond the sharing window.

threats: Stalking: recipient saves location stream outside the TTL window.
Server-side surveillance: unbounded retention of location history.
Inference attacks: correlation of speed/direction with home/work addresses.
Replay: old location updates replayed after session expires.
mitigations: Location updates stored in Redis only (TTL-bound, no PostgreSQL persistence by default). No durable location log.
Each update packet signed with HMAC-SHA256(session_key, lat|lon|ts) — server validates before broadcast. Prevents spoofed location injection.
Replay protection: each update includes monotonically increasing sequence_number per session; server rejects seq ≤ last_accepted.
After TTL expiry or stop: Redis key deleted immediately, no further updates broadcast.
Optional: user can configure 'approximate location' (±500m fuzzing via Gaussian noise applied server-side).

update_protocol.transport: WebSocket (preferred) or HTTP polling fallback (30s interval)
update_protocol.update_rate: Client sends every 5s minimum (server throttles more frequent updates). Battery/accuracy trade-off configuration.
server_pipeline: 1. Receive update via WS message type: LIVE_LOCATION_UPDATE
3. Load session from Redis → verify not expired, sequence_number valid
6. Atomically SET new value in Redis + EXPIRE refresh
7. Fanout via pub/sub to all subscribers of conversation_id

stop_sharing.explicit: User taps 'Stop' → DELETE Redis key → UPDATE sessions.stopped_at → emit LIVE_LOCATION_STOPPED event
stop_sharing.ttl_expiry: Redis key expires → server detects via keyspace notifications → emit LIVE_LOCATION_EXPIRED event

scalability.fanout: Redis pub/sub channel per conversation_id. At 10M users, expected active live-location sessions: ~0.01% = 1000 concurrent. Trivially handled by single Redis cluster.
scalability.update_frequency: 5s × 1000 sessions = 200 updates/s. Sub-millisecond Redis writes. No DB write per update.

ui.sender_view: Active sharing indicator in composer area with countdown timer. 'Stop Sharing' button.
ui.recipient_view: Map card in conversation with animated pin. Shows heading arrow if available. Tapping opens full-screen map.
ui.expired_state: Pin becomes grey, label shows 'Location expired {time_ago}'
